statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.inputFormat: The input will consist of a single line containing a matrix as a 2D vector of integers, where each inner vector represents a row of the grid. The matrix will contain non-negative integers between 0 and 6, inclusive. The grid is guaranteed to have a valid path from the top-left cell to the bottom-right cell.
statement.outputFormat: The output will be a single integer representing the number of unique paths in the grid.
statement.notes: Two cells are the same if they have the same coordinates and the same value.

hints.text: The problem is trying to calculate the number of unique paths in the grid.

editorial.title: Incorrect Memoization in Grid Paths
editorial.intuition: We should use dynamic programming to solve this problem.
editorial.bruteForce: We can use a brute-force approach by iterating over all possible paths in the grid and counting them.
editorial.optimalSolution: We can use a 2D array to store the number of unique paths from each cell to the top-left cell.
commonMistakes: Using a single variable to store the total number of unique paths
Not handling the base case correctly
